title: HeLa cryopreservation - trehalose vs DMSO
hypothesis: Replacing sucrose with trehalose as a cryoprotectant in the freezing medium will increase post-thaw viability of HeLa cells by at least 15 percentage points compared to the standard DMSO protocol, due to trehalose's superior membrane stabilization at low temperatures.
notes: Best demo hypothesis. Concrete, visual, budget realistic (~EUR 5,860). Uses HeLa + trehalose from catalog.

keyParameters.primaryEndpoint: Post-thaw cell viability (%) at 24h by trypan blue exclusion
keyParameters.statisticalDesign: n=12 independent freeze-thaw cycles per condition, paired t-test or Mann-Whitney U

indispensable: Cell viability counting at 24h, 48h, 72h post-thaw (trypan blue exclusion, hemocytometer or automated counter)
Controlled-rate cryopreservation at -1°C/min (Mr. Frosty or programmatic controlled-rate freezer)
Long-term storage in liquid nitrogen (≥1 week before thaw)
Statistical comparison across ≥12 replicates per condition (paired t-test or Mann-Whitney U, p<0.05 threshold)
recommended: Post-thaw proliferation curve to assess recovery doubling time (72h culture after thaw)
Membrane integrity assay by flow cytometry (LIVE/DEAD Fixable Aqua or calcein AM + PI dual staining)
Pre-freeze viability confirmation (>95% baseline required for valid experiment)
optional: Metabolic activity assay (CellTiter-Glo ATP luminescence) as orthogonal viability endpoint
Early apoptosis profiling (Annexin V / caspase-3 activation at 4h post-thaw)
Electron microscopy for ultrastructural membrane damage assessment
DSC (differential scanning calorimetry) to characterize trehalose glass transition temperature

indispensable: CO2 incubator (37°C, 5% CO2, humidified)
Biosafety cabinet class II (BSL-1)
Hemocytometer or automated cell counter (e.g., Countess II)
Mr. Frosty or controlled-rate freezer (-1°C/min to -80°C)
Liquid nitrogen storage dewar (long-term cell storage)
-80°C freezer (intermediate storage before LN2 transfer)
recommended: Flow cytometer with 488 nm and 405 nm lasers (for LIVE/DEAD / calcein-AM / PI staining)
Inverted light microscope (cell morphology assessment)
Microplate reader (fluorescence mode, for calcein-AM/PI assay)
optional: Confocal microscope (membrane integrity imaging)
Luminometer or plate reader with luminescence module (CellTiter-Glo)

indispensable: Target cell line (HeLa CCL-2 ATCC or equivalent, passage <30)
Experimental cryoprotectant: trehalose dihydrate 99% (0.5–2 M stock in PBS, filter-sterile)
Control cryoprotectant: DMSO cell-culture grade (10% v/v final in complete medium)
Complete culture medium: DMEM high glucose + 10% FBS + 1% Pen-Strep
Cell dissociation: Trypsin-EDTA 0.25% (harvest before cryopreservation)
Viability dye: Trypan Blue 0.4% (primary readout, 1:1 mix with cells)
recommended: Cryovials 1.8 mL sterile (externally threaded for LN2)
PBS pH 7.4 (washing steps, cryoprotectant stock preparation)
L-Glutamine 200 mM (supplement for extended post-thaw culture)
Sterile syringe filters 0.22 µm (sterilize trehalose stock)
96-well TC-treated plates (post-thaw proliferation assay)
optional: LIVE/DEAD Fixable Aqua kit (flow cytometry, more precise than trypan for low-viability samples)
CellTiter-Glo (ATP-based metabolic activity, orthogonal endpoint)
Annexin V / PI apoptosis kit (early apoptosis at 4h post-thaw)
LDH cytotoxicity assay (membrane damage marker, colorimetric)

title: LGG probiotic gut permeability in C57BL/6 mice
hypothesis: Supplementing C57BL/6 mice with Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG for 4 weeks will reduce intestinal permeability by at least 30% compared to controls, measured by FITC-dextran assay, due to upregulation of tight junction proteins claudin-1 and occludin.
notes: Strong secondary demo. Well-established field, Tavily will find many references.

keyParameters.primaryEndpoint: Serum FITC-dextran (relative fluorescence units) at 4h post-gavage
keyParameters.minimumEffectSize: ≥30% reduction in FITC-dextran permeability vs PBS control
keyParameters.statisticalDesign: n=10 per group (control vs LGG), one-way ANOVA + Tukey post-hoc

indispensable: FITC-dextran intestinal permeability assay (600 mg/kg oral gavage, serum collected at 4h, fluorescence at 485/528 nm)
Oral gavage for daily probiotic dosing (1×10⁹ CFU LGG in 200 µL PBS, 20G needle)
qPCR for intestinal claudin-1 (Cldn1) and occludin (Ocln) mRNA from jejunum/colon tissue
CFU plating on MRS agar to verify LGG viability in gavage stock on each dosing day
recommended: Western blot (claudin-1 and occludin protein, intestinal lysate from RIPA buffer extraction)
H&E histology of intestinal sections (assess villus height, crypt depth, epithelial integrity)
Mouse IL-6 ELISA (serum cytokine as systemic inflammatory marker)
Body weight monitoring twice weekly (welfare indicator and treatment tolerability)
optional: 16S rRNA gut microbiome sequencing of caecal content (stool day 0, 14, 28)
Mouse TNF-alpha ELISA (additional inflammatory cytokine panel)
Immunofluorescence for claudin-1 and occludin localization in tissue sections
Mucosal secretory IgA (sIgA) ELISA
TEER measurement in ex vivo Ussing chamber

indispensable: Fluorescence microplate reader (485 nm excitation / 528 nm emission for FITC-dextran)
qPCR thermocycler with real-time fluorescence detection
Biosafety cabinet (LGG preparation and gavage dosing)
Anaerobic workstation or jar (LGG culture at 37°C)
Tissue homogenizer (intestinal tissue for RNA/protein extraction)
Microcentrifuge and refrigerated centrifuge
recommended: Western blot system with transfer apparatus and ChemiDoc imager
Cryostat or paraffin embedding station (histology sections)
Bright-field microscope (H&E slide review)
Plate reader (ELISA absorbance at 450 nm)
optional: Confocal microscope (immunofluorescence localization)
Ussing chamber system (ex vivo permeability measurement)
16S sequencing service (outsourced or in-house Illumina MiSeq)

indispensable: Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG ATCC 53103 (reference strain, 1×10⁹ CFU/day gavage dose)
C57BL/6J mice male 8-10 weeks (minimum 10 per group + 3 extra per group for attrition)
FITC-Dextran 4 kDa (intestinal permeability tracer, 600 mg/kg oral)
MRS Broth (LGG culture medium, anaerobic 37°C)
PBS pH 7.4 (probiotic suspension vehicle)
RNeasy Mini Kit (intestinal tissue RNA extraction for qPCR)
iScript cDNA Synthesis Kit + SYBR Green Supermix
Mouse Claudin-1 qPCR primer pair (primary target gene)
Mouse GAPDH qPCR primer pair (housekeeping normalization)
Rodent standard chow 2018S (control diet)
recommended: RIPA lysis buffer + Halt protease inhibitor (protein extraction for WB)
Anti-Claudin-1 rabbit monoclonal antibody (WB 1:1000)
Anti-rabbit HRP secondary + ECL substrate + NuPAGE gels (WB detection)
H&E staining kit + 10% neutral buffered formalin (histology)
Mouse IL-6 ELISA kit (serum inflammatory marker)
MRS Agar plates (CFU counting of LGG gavage stock)
Sterile gavage needles 20G × 50 mm (oral dosing)
Micro blood collection tubes EDTA 0.5 mL (serum collection)
Mouse Occludin qPCR primer pair (secondary tight junction marker)
optional: ZymoBIOMICS DNA Miniprep Kit (16S stool microbiome)
Mouse TNF-alpha ELISA kit (additional cytokine)
Anti-Occludin antibody (WB/IF)
Goat anti-rabbit Alexa Fluor 488 + ProLong Gold DAPI (IF)
Mouse sIgA ELISA kit

title: CRP paper biosensor below 0.5 mg/L in whole blood
hypothesis: A paper-based electrochemical biosensor functionalized with anti-CRP antibodies will detect C-reactive protein in whole blood at concentrations below 0.5 mg/L within 10 minutes, matching laboratory ELISA sensitivity without requiring sample preprocessing.
notes: Good for showing electrochemical domain. Budget higher (~EUR 8,000). Complex protocol but impressive.

keyParameters.primaryEndpoint: Limit of detection (LOD) for CRP in spiked whole blood (mg/L)
keyParameters.statisticalDesign: LOD = mean blank + 3σ (n=10 replicates per concentration), validated in ≥5 independent donor blood samples

indispensable: Screen-printed electrode functionalization with EDC/NHS carbodiimide coupling chemistry (anti-CRP antibody immobilization)
BSA blocking of non-specific binding sites (1% BSA in PBS, 30 min RT)
Electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS) for each fabrication step (ferri/ferrocyanide redox probe, 5 mM, 0.1-100 kHz)
Amperometric or DPV detection of CRP in spiked matrix (0.01–10 mg/L calibration curve)
LOD and LOQ calculation (blank + 3σ and blank + 10σ, n=10)
Reference ELISA validation (high-sensitivity CRP ELISA on same samples)
recommended: Cyclic voltammetry (CV) for electrode surface characterization before and after modification
Gold nanoparticle signal amplification (AuNP-antibody conjugate on detection antibody)
Nafion 5% protective membrane layer (electrode stability in blood matrix)
Scanning electron microscopy (SEM) of electrode surface (optional morphology confirmation)
Cross-reactivity panel (albumin, IgG, fibrinogen at 10× physiological concentration)
optional: Differential pulse voltammetry (DPV) as alternative to EIS for signal readout
Atomic force microscopy (AFM) surface roughness characterization
Shelf-life stability study (sensors stored at 4°C, tested at day 0, 7, 14, 30)
Selectivity testing against panel of 10 serum proteins
Clinical sample validation (certified CRP reference serum, JCTLM-listed method comparison)

indispensable: Potentiostat / impedance analyzer (e.g., Biologic SP-50 or PalmSens4) with EIS capability
3-electrode cell setup (working = modified SPE, reference = Ag/AgCl, counter = Pt wire)
Micropipettes 0.5–1000 µL + tips
pH meter (buffer and electrolyte verification)
Analytical balance (reagent preparation)
recommended: Scanning electron microscope (electrode surface morphology)
UV-Vis spectrophotometer (AuNP conjugation verification at 520 nm)
Fluorescence plate reader (if parallel ELISA validation on same plate)
Fume hood (EDC/NHS coupling in phosphate buffer)
optional: Atomic force microscope (layer-by-layer surface characterization)
Dynamic light scattering (DLS for AuNP size verification)
Plasma cleaner (surface activation before functionalization)

indispensable: Screen-printed carbon electrode strips (3-electrode, TE100 or equivalent)
Anti-CRP monoclonal antibody for surface functionalization (capture)
EDC (1-ethyl-3-(3-dimethylaminopropyl)carbodiimide HCl) — prepare fresh
NHS (N-hydroxysuccinimide) — use with EDC for stable amide bond
BSA fraction V (blocking agent, 1% in PBS)
Human CRP recombinant protein (calibration standard, 0.01–10 mg/L serial dilutions)
Potassium ferricyanide + potassium ferrocyanide (5 mM each, redox probe for EIS/CV)
Potassium chloride 0.1 M (supporting electrolyte)
Pooled human EDTA whole blood (matrix validation, spiked with known CRP concentrations)
CRP high-sensitivity ELISA kit (gold standard reference method)
Whatman Grade 4 filter paper (biosensor substrate)
recommended: Gold nanoparticles 20 nm citrate-capped (signal amplification for sub-0.5 mg/L LOD)
Nafion 5% solution (protective membrane for whole-blood stability)
PBS pH 7.4 (all washing and blocking steps)
Ultrapure MilliQ water (all buffer preparation)
optional: Ferrocene methanol (redox reference standard, electrochemical calibration)
Sulfo-NHS (water-soluble NHS ester for aqueous coupling)
Ethanolamine (quenching excess NHS esters after coupling)

title: Sporomusa ovata CO2 to acetate at -400 mV vs SHE
hypothesis: Introducing Sporomusa ovata into a bioelectrochemical system at a cathode potential of -400mV vs SHE will fix CO2 into acetate at a rate of at least 150 mmol/L/day, outperforming current biocatalytic carbon capture benchmarks by at least 20%.
notes: Climate domain. Directly from brief. Specialist reagents available in catalog.

keyParameters.primaryEndpoint: Acetate production rate (mmol/L/day) by enzymatic assay or HPLC
keyParameters.statisticalDesign: n=3 BES reactors per condition, Coulombic efficiency calculated from charge passed
keyParameters.experimentDuration: 8 weeks (BES construction, inoculation, steady-state operation, analysis)

indispensable: Chronoamperometry at fixed cathode potential (-400 mV vs SHE = -597 mV vs Ag/AgCl sat. KCl)
CO2 sparging of catholyte (99.9% CO2 at 20 mL/min, continuous pH monitoring)
Acetate quantification by enzymatic assay (Megazyme) or HPLC (Aminex HPX-87H column)
Coulombic efficiency calculation (moles acetate per moles electrons delivered)
Anaerobic technique throughout (glovebox or Schlenk-line for all manipulations)
recommended: HPLC organic acid profiling (acetate, formate, succinate — Aminex HPX-87H, 5 mM H2SO4 mobile phase)
SEM imaging of biocathode surface (biofilm visualization before and after experiment)
Current density vs time trace (chronoamperometry, assess biofilm establishment phase)
16S rRNA quantification of Sporomusa on cathode (qPCR with genus-specific primers)
optional: EIS of biocathode (charge transfer resistance, biofilm capacitance)
Scanning electron microscopy with EDX (elemental mapping of biofilm)
LC-MS metabolomics (identify minor co-products)
Gas chromatography (H2 and CO2 headspace analysis, quantify gas conversion)
Hydrogen control experiment (replace CO2 with H2 as electron donor baseline)

indispensable: Potentiostat / galvanostat with EIS module (Biologic SP-50 or equivalent)
Anaerobic glovebox or anaerobic chamber (O2 < 0.5 ppm for all manipulations)
H-cell BES reactor (borosilicate glass, two-chamber separated by Nafion 117 membrane)
CO2 sparging system (mass flow controller + tubing + sparger stone)
pH controller or meter (maintain catholyte pH 7.0 ± 0.2)
Autoclave (sterilize all glassware and media)
recommended: HPLC system with Aminex HPX-87H column + RI detector (organic acid quantification)
SEM (biocathode biofilm imaging)
Thermostated water bath or heating jacket (37°C BES operation)
Gas-tight sampling syringes (headspace analysis)
optional: GC-MS (headspace H2 and CO2 quantification)
LC-MS (metabolomics for minor co-products)
Fluorescence microscope with DAPI + live/dead staining (planktonic cells vs biofilm)

indispensable: Sporomusa ovata DSM 2662 (DSMZ ampoule — strictly anaerobic acetogen)
Carbon cloth electrode AvCarb P50 (high surface-area biocathode)
Titanium wire 0.5 mm (corrosion-resistant current collector)
Ag/AgCl reference electrode saturated KCl (+197 mV vs SHE)
Platinum wire counter electrode (inert anode)
Nafion 117 proton-exchange membrane (pre-treated in 5% H2SO4, then ultrapure water)
CO2 gas cylinder 99.9% (continuous sparging of catholyte at 20 mL/min)
Megazyme Acetic Acid Assay Kit (primary acetate quantification)
HEPES buffer 1 M (pH maintenance in BES medium, 50 mM final)
Mineral salts for Sporomusa growth medium (NH4Cl, K2HPO4, MgSO4, CaCl2, trace elements)
recommended: Indigo carmine (redox indicator for anaerobic medium quality)
Resazurin sodium salt (anaerobic viability indicator)
Bio-Rad Aminex HPX-87H column (HPLC organic acid profiling)
Sodium bicarbonate (buffer/CO2 source when gas sparging unavailable)
Hydrogen gas cylinder 99.9% (H2 control experiment)
optional: Nafion 5% solution (electrode coating for EIS measurements)
Gas-tight septa and crimp vials (headspace sampling)
LC-MS grade solvents (metabolomics)

title: Claudin-1 overexpression reduces paracellular permeability in Caco-2 cells
hypothesis: Lentiviral overexpression of claudin-1 in Caco-2 cells will reduce FITC-dextran (4 kDa) paracellular flux by at least 40% compared to empty-vector controls, confirming claudin-1 as a primary determinant of intestinal barrier function.
notes: Good for demonstrating 'exact match' novelty signal. Well-studied topic.

keyParameters.primaryEndpoint: FITC-dextran (4 kDa) paracellular flux across Transwell monolayer (apical-to-basolateral)
keyParameters.minimumEffectSize: ≥40% reduction in FITC-dextran permeability coefficient (Papp) vs empty-vector control
keyParameters.statisticalDesign: n=6 Transwell inserts per condition (3 independent passages), Student's t-test, p<0.05

indispensable: Caco-2 differentiation on Transwell inserts (seed 5×10⁴ cells/insert, differentiate 21 days post-confluence)
TEER monitoring (trans-epithelial electrical resistance, Millicell ERS-2, twice per week — accept >300 Ω·cm² before assay)
FITC-dextran 4 kDa paracellular permeability assay (1 mg/mL apical, 2h incubation, basolateral sampling)
Lentiviral transduction of CLDN1 cDNA (MOI 5-10, polybrene 8 µg/mL, puromycin selection 72h post-transduction)
qPCR validation of claudin-1 overexpression (human CLDN1 primers, GAPDH normalization)
recommended: Western blot (claudin-1 protein, RIPA lysate from insert monolayer, anti-claudin-1 EPR7786 1:1000)
Immunofluorescence for junctional claudin-1 localization (confocal, ZO-1 co-staining for TJ marker)
ZO-1 (tight junction scaffold) staining by IF to confirm intact junctional complex
Cell proliferation assay (CellTiter-Glo) to rule out overexpression affecting growth rate
optional: Sodium fluorescein (376 Da) paracellular marker for small molecule flux comparison
TRITC-dextran 70 kDa (transcytosis marker, should not change with claudin-1 overexpression)
RNA-sequencing of overexpression cells (off-target transcriptional changes)
Proteasome inhibitor controls (MG132) to verify protein turnover
Rescue experiment (CLDN1 shRNA knockdown after overexpression)

indispensable: CO2 incubator (37°C, 5% CO2, for 21-day Caco-2 differentiation)
Millicell ERS-2 TEER meter + STX01 electrode (barrier integrity monitoring)
Fluorescence plate reader (485/528 nm for FITC-dextran Papp calculation)
Biosafety cabinet class II (lentiviral work requires BSL-2)
Microcentrifuge and refrigerated centrifuge (lentiviral concentration)
recommended: Confocal microscope (claudin-1 and ZO-1 junctional localization)
Western blot system + ChemiDoc imager (claudin-1 protein expression)
qPCR thermocycler (CLDN1 mRNA overexpression validation)
optional: Ultracentrifuge (high-titer lentivirus concentration by ultracentrifugation)
Luminometer / plate reader luminescence (CellTiter-Glo growth assay)

indispensable: Caco-2 cells ATCC HTB-37 (intestinal barrier model, 21-day differentiation on Transwell)
Corning Transwell polycarbonate inserts 0.4 µm pore, 24-well format
Millicell ERS-2 TEER meter (barrier integrity, must be >300 Ω·cm² before assay)
EMEM + 20% FBS (Caco-2 recommended complete medium per ATCC)
FITC-Dextran 4 kDa (paracellular tracer, 1 mg/mL apical)
Human Claudin-1 qPCR primer pair (overexpression validation)
Human GAPDH qPCR primer pair (housekeeping normalization)
Lentiviral vector encoding human CLDN1 cDNA (Addgene or custom, with puromycin selection marker)
Polybrene (transduction efficiency enhancer, 8 µg/mL)
Puromycin (selection antibiotic for stably transduced cells, 2-5 µg/mL)
recommended: Anti-Claudin-1 rabbit monoclonal antibody EPR7786 (WB 1:1000, IF 1:200)
RIPA lysis buffer + Halt Protease Inhibitor (protein extraction)
NuPAGE 4-12% Bis-Tris gels + ECL substrate (WB)
Goat anti-rabbit Alexa Fluor 488 secondary (IF confocal)
ZO-1 antibody (tight junction scaffold marker for IF)
PBS pH 7.4 (all washing steps)
optional: Sodium fluorescein (small-molecule flux control)
TRITC-dextran 70 kDa (transcytosis marker control)
CellTiter-Glo (proliferation rate control)
shRNA claudin-1 construct (knockdown rescue experiment)

title: High-dose probiotic cocktail reduces LPS-induced systemic inflammation in mice
hypothesis: A 4-strain probiotic cocktail (LGG, L. acidophilus, B. longum, B. breve) dosed daily for 3 weeks prior to LPS challenge will reduce serum IL-6 by at least 50% and TNF-alpha by 40% compared to LPS-challenged controls, via gut-barrier-mediated endotoxin neutralization.
notes: Good for demonstrating 'novel' signal - specific 4-strain combination less studied.

keyParameters.minimumEffectSize: ≥50% reduction in IL-6 vs LPS-only control; ≥40% reduction in TNF-alpha
keyParameters.statisticalDesign: 4 groups (naive, LPS-only, probiotic-only, probiotic+LPS), n=10 per group, one-way ANOVA + Tukey
keyParameters.experimentDuration: 10 weeks (3 weeks probiotic + LPS challenge + analysis)

indispensable: Daily gavage of 4-strain cocktail (LGG + L. acidophilus + B. longum + B. breve, 2.5×10⁸ CFU each, total 1×10⁹/day) for 21 days
IP LPS challenge at day 21 (E. coli O111:B4, 5 mg/kg body weight in sterile PBS)
Serum collection at 6h post-LPS (cardiac puncture under terminal anaesthesia)
Mouse IL-6 ELISA (serum, primary endpoint, LLOQ 4 pg/mL)
Mouse TNF-alpha ELISA (serum, co-primary endpoint)
FITC-dextran permeability assay at day 21 pre-challenge (intestinal barrier baseline)
recommended: CFU plating on selective media to verify each strain viability in daily gavage preparation
Intestinal FITC-dextran permeability (to confirm barrier improvement as mechanism)
qPCR for intestinal claudin-1 and occludin mRNA (tight junction expression)
Serum LPS-binding protein (LBP) ELISA (endotoxin translocation marker)
Body weight and clinical score monitoring throughout (welfare and efficacy tracking)
optional: 16S rRNA microbiome sequencing (caecal content day 0, 14, 21 — does the cocktail engraft?)
Serum IFN-gamma and IL-10 (Th1/regulatory cytokine balance)
Colon histology H&E (mucus layer integrity, goblet cell number)
Mouse MCP-1/CCL2 ELISA (monocyte recruitment marker)
Faecal calprotectin (intestinal inflammation marker, non-invasive time-course)

indispensable: Anaerobic workstation (Bifidobacterium cultures — strictly anaerobic)
Microplate reader (fluorescence for FITC-dextran, absorbance for ELISA)
Biosafety cabinet class II (LPS handling — endotoxin precautions)
Centrifuge (serum separation 2000 × g, 15 min)
Gavage needles 20G and 22G (daily dosing)
Colony counter (CFU plates)
recommended: qPCR thermocycler (claudin-1, occludin mRNA expression)
Tissue homogenizer (intestinal tissue lysis for RNA/protein)
Histology equipment (cryostat or paraffin embedder + microtome)
optional: Illumina MiSeq or outsourced 16S sequencing
Flow cytometer (peripheral blood lymphocyte subset analysis)
Confocal microscope (immunofluorescence histology)

indispensable: Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG ATCC 53103 (Strain 1 of cocktail)
Lactobacillus acidophilus ATCC 4356 (Strain 2 of cocktail)
Bifidobacterium longum ATCC 15707 (Strain 3 of cocktail)
Bifidobacterium breve ATCC 15700 (Strain 4 of cocktail)
LPS from E. coli O111:B4 (systemic inflammatory challenge, 5 mg/kg IP)
C57BL/6J mice, male, 8-10 weeks (≥13 per group accounting for attrition)
MRS Broth (Lactobacillus culture medium)
Mouse IL-6 ELISA kit (primary endpoint, LLOQ 4 pg/mL)
Mouse TNF-alpha ELISA kit (co-primary endpoint)
FITC-Dextran 4 kDa (barrier function pre-challenge)
Sterile gavage needles 20G (daily dosing)
recommended: MRS Agar plates (CFU verification of each strain daily)
PBS pH 7.4 (vehicle for probiotic suspension and LPS injection)
RNeasy Mini Kit + iScript + SYBR Green (qPCR for tight junction genes)
Mouse Claudin-1 + GAPDH qPCR primers
RIPA buffer + protease inhibitor (protein extraction if WB included)
Micro blood collection tubes EDTA (cardiac puncture serum)
Rodent standard chow 2018S
optional: ZymoBIOMICS DNA Miniprep Kit (16S microbiome stool samples)
Mouse IFN-gamma ELISA kit
Mouse IL-10 ELISA kit
H&E staining kit (intestinal histology)
10% neutral buffered formalin (tissue fixation)

title: Microfluidic antibody capture improves CRP detection speed to 5 minutes
hypothesis: Integrating a microfluidic channel with 3D-printed laminar flow into a paper-based CRP biosensor will reduce sample-to-result time from 10 to 5 minutes while maintaining a LOD below 0.5 mg/L, by concentrating analyte at the electrode surface.
notes: Stretch hypothesis showing novelty detection works for genuinely novel ideas.

keyParameters.minimumEffectSize: Time reduced from 10 min to ≤ 5 min at maintained LOD ≤ 0.5 mg/L vs non-microfluidic control
keyParameters.statisticalDesign: LOD = blank + 3σ (n=10), time-to-result for amperometric plateau across n=8 sensors
keyParameters.experimentDuration: 12 weeks (chip design + PDMS fabrication + biosensor integration + validation)

indispensable: PDMS microfluidic chip fabrication (SU-8 or 3D-printed mold, Sylgard 184 10:1 ratio, cure 1h at 70°C)
Plasma treatment for PDMS-glass/paper bonding (O2 plasma, 30W, 60s)
SPE functionalization with EDC/NHS + anti-CRP antibody (as per HYPO-003 baseline)
Syringe-pump or capillary-driven flow characterization (flow rate optimization for 5 min assay)
Amperometric current response at optimized flow rate (chronoamperometry, -0.2 V vs Ag/AgCl)
LOD calculation in spiked whole blood (blank + 3σ, n=10 per concentration)
Head-to-head comparison vs static incubation 10 min (non-microfluidic control)
recommended: Microfluidic channel dimension optimization by COMSOL simulation (Reynolds number, analyte residence time)
FITC-dextran flow visualization (fluorescence microscopy, confirm laminar flow profile)
EIS characterization of microfluidic-integrated electrode (ferri/ferrocyanide probe)
Non-specific binding test in microfluidic format (1% BSA, 10% whole blood matrix)
optional: 3D-printed integrated potentiostat housing (point-of-care integration proof-of-concept)
Pressure-driven vs electro-osmotic flow comparison
Multiplexed CRP + troponin dual-analyte chip design
Clinical sample validation (certified clinical residual sera, n=20)

indispensable: Potentiostat with EIS capability (Biologic SP-50 or PalmSens4)
Plasma cleaner (O2 plasma bonding of PDMS to electrode substrate)
Syringe pump (controlled flow rate 1–20 µL/min for 5 min assay optimization)
Biosafety cabinet (blood sample handling)
Stereomicroscope or optical microscope (chip alignment and bonding inspection)
recommended: Fluorescence microscope (FITC-dextran flow visualization for channel characterization)
SEM (electrode surface after microfluidic integration)
3D printer with resin (rapid mold prototyping — SLA resin at 50 µm resolution)
optional: Soft-lithography spin coater + SU-8 photoresist kit (high-resolution mold fabrication)
COMSOL Multiphysics simulation license (flow and mass transport modelling)

indispensable: PDMS Sylgard 184 kit (microfluidic chip material, 10:1 base:curing agent)
Screen-printed carbon electrode strips (biosensor working electrode)
Anti-CRP monoclonal antibody (surface functionalization, same as HYPO-003)
EDC + NHS (antibody coupling chemistry)
BSA 1% (non-specific blocking)
Human CRP recombinant protein (calibration standards 0.01–10 mg/L)
Potassium ferricyanide + ferrocyanide (EIS redox probe)
Pooled human EDTA whole blood (matrix validation)
Syringe pump and PTFE tubing (flow control)
recommended: Gold nanoparticles 20 nm (signal amplification at short assay time)
PBS + KCl (electrolyte and wash buffer)
Ferrocene methanol (electrochemical reference calibration)
Ultrapure water (buffer preparation)
optional: SU-8 photoresist 2050 (alternative lithographic mold for <50 µm channels)
Trichloro(1H,1H,2H,2H-perfluorooctyl)silane (mold release agent for PDMS casting)

title: Succinic acid as co-product in Sporomusa BES system
hypothesis: Modifying the catholyte pH from 7.0 to 5.5 in a Sporomusa ovata bioelectrochemical system will shift the product distribution from acetate (dominant at pH 7) to include succinate at >20% molar yield, exploiting the pH-dependent fumarate reductase pathway.
notes: Advanced climate hypothesis. Less studied. Good for 'novel' signal demonstration.

keyParameters.primaryEndpoint: Succinate molar yield (%) as fraction of total organic acid products at pH 5.5 vs pH 7.0
keyParameters.minimumEffectSize: >20% succinate molar yield at pH 5.5 vs <5% at pH 7.0
keyParameters.statisticalDesign: n=3 BES reactors per pH condition, HPLC product quantification at 48h intervals, two-way ANOVA
keyParameters.experimentDuration: 12 weeks (BES setup + pH screening + steady-state operation)

indispensable: pH-controlled BES operation at two conditions (pH 5.5 and pH 7.0, automatic pH-stat or manual adjustment)
HPLC organic acid profiling for acetate, succinate, and formate (Aminex HPX-87H, 5 mM H2SO4, 0.6 mL/min, 35°C, RI detector)
Succinate and acetate external calibration curves (0.1–50 mM standards in mobile phase)
Chronoamperometry at -400 mV vs SHE (consistent cathode potential across both pH conditions)
CO2 sparging (continuous, 20 mL/min) to maintain carbon source availability at both pH values
Coulombic efficiency calculation (moles total product / moles electrons passed)
recommended: pH-stat system (titration pump for automated pH control) or pH-feedback potentiostat
Fumarate addition control experiment (exogenous fumarate at 10 mM to verify fumarate reductase pathway)
16S rRNA qPCR on catholyte samples (Sporomusa viability across pH conditions)
SEM of biocathode at end-point (biofilm integrity at acidic pH)
optional: Transcriptomic analysis of Sporomusa at pH 5.5 vs 7.0 (fumarate reductase gene expression)
GC headspace analysis (CO2 consumption rate by mass balance)
LC-MS metabolomics (minor pathway products)
Adaptive laboratory evolution (serial passage at pH 5.5 to select for succinate producers)
In silico flux balance analysis (genome-scale metabolic model of Sporomusa)

indispensable: Potentiostat / galvanostat with EIS module (Biologic SP-50 or equivalent)
Anaerobic glovebox (O2 < 1 ppm — pH 5.5 is particularly stressful, strict anaerobiosis critical)
H-cell BES reactor (borosilicate glass, Nafion 117 membrane separated)
HPLC system with Aminex HPX-87H + RI detector (organic acid profiling)
pH meter with electrode (catholyte monitoring)
CO2 sparging system (mass flow controller, pH-independent carbon source)
recommended: pH-stat controller (automated titrant addition to maintain target pH)
Autoclave (sterilize all media and glassware)
SEM (biocathode biofilm integrity at acidic pH)
qPCR thermocycler (Sporomusa 16S viability)
optional: GC-MS (headspace CO2/H2 quantification)
Illumina sequencing service (RNA-seq or metagenomics)
COMSOL or MATLAB (mass transfer modelling at different pH)

indispensable: Sporomusa ovata DSM 2662 (DSMZ — strictly anaerobic acetogen with fumarate reductase activity)
Bio-Rad Aminex HPX-87H HPLC column (organic acid profiling — required for succinate vs acetate distinction)
Succinic acid disodium salt (HPLC calibration standard, 0.1–50 mM)
Acetic acid / sodium acetate (HPLC calibration standard)
Carbon cloth electrode AvCarb P50 + titanium wire + Ag/AgCl reference + Pt counter
Nafion 117 PEM (pre-treated)
CO2 gas cylinder 99.9% (primary carbon source)
pH buffer components (citrate-phosphate or MES for pH 5.5, HEPES for pH 7.0)
Megazyme Acetic Acid Assay Kit (complementary enzymatic check of acetate)
recommended: Fumaric acid sodium salt (exogenous substrate for pathway verification control)
Resazurin and indigo carmine (anaerobic medium quality indicators)
Sodium bicarbonate NaHCO3 (supplemental CO2 source when sparging disrupts pH control)
Mineral salts for BES medium (NH4Cl, K2HPO4, MgSO4, CaCl2, trace element solution)
Hydrogen gas 99.9% (H2 control experiment at both pH values)
optional: RNA extraction kit (transcriptomics for fumarate reductase gene expression)
Species-specific qPCR primers for Sporomusa (biomass quantification)
LC-MS grade acetonitrile and formic acid (metabolomics mobile phase)
